channel.title: general on Analyst
channel.description: Recent content in general on Analyst

item.title: Apple Encryption
item.description: The main idea behind the scenes is that applications or even OS itself doesn’t know the keys.

item.title: Apple Mach-O File
item.description: Intro Using otool we can get general info about the Mach-O file: otool -h [app_name] > Mach header magic cputype cpusubtype caps filetype ncmds sizeofcmds flags 0xfeedfacf 16777228 0 0x00 2 115 11384 0x00218085 Quick refernce on the meaning:

item.title: Memory Addressing
item.description: I’ve had a lot of time to dive into certain pecularities of how programs are outlined both in RAM and on disk when working as a malware analyst.

item.title: A Few Good Bits
item.description: Saturday I’ve recently have had a severe poisoning (I only hope it’s not the rotavirus 😨) and I’ve spent one evening and one day barely moving.

item.title: App Transport Security
item.description: In this article I’m trying to untangle a very confusing notion for some people (myself included) - App Transport Security.

item.title: Apple LAContext vs User Presence
item.description: In this article I’n going to research this: how LAContext is different from User Presence, in what way it’s better.

item.title: HDD Overview
item.description: In this article I’m going to give a general overview of how HDDs work. Floppy Floppy disks 💾 are like dinosaurs 🦕.

item.title: Calling Conventions in x86
item.description: In this article I’m giving an overview of different calling conventions with examples. Intro Consider the following code snippet:

item.title: Flags
item.description: CF ZF OF

item.title: Operations
item.description: In this article I’m describing all assembly operations that I’ve encountered myseld and also wasn’t lazy anough to put down an explanation about here.

item.title: References
item.description: Here are all books, articles, websites, that I used to write in this section. If I use references in each article, I sometimes forget to mention someone’s impact, so I’ve decided to make a separate doc with all the references.

item.title: Assembly Intro
item.description: In this article I’m giving a quick dive-in into assembly. It’s very simple and very difficult at the same time.

item.title: BTFM for Reverse Engineering
item.description: Rabin2 rabin2 -I [progname] # main info about the prog rabin2 -z [progname] # the strings from the binary's data section rabin2 -zz [progname] # all strings from the binary rabin2 -zqq [progname] # strings only without additional info R2 r2 [progname] # launch radare2 > s main # seak main function and go to it > aa # find all strings > v # psedu GUI mode (Hex editor) > VV # graph mode > afvn user_input input # rename local var and all the places it was referenced # rename function > s function_old_name > afn function_new_name > c # show cursor in visual mode > afvd # print all local variables Graph mode VV # open Graph # go to a function via shortcut (;[ao]) o # first type 'o' key > a # then enter the second letter from the brakets Test this https://r2wiki.

item.title: iOS Introduction
item.description: Low Level Security UID - key that is fused into Application processor. No one know it and can get from the device.

item.title: Android Applications Intro
item.description: Android Overview I’m not going to describe in all the details the basics of this architecture for it’s described pretty good here: Mobile Security Guide link.

item.title: iOS Booting Process
item.description: When an iDevice is started, two separate booting processes are taking place: booting of Application Processor and booting of Secure Enclave.

item.title: iOS Structure
item.description: When installed on the device, *.ipa is unzipped into a corresponding folder. That’s the contents:

item.title: iDevice Internals
item.description: This article is about device internals for different versions of iDevices. This can be helpful for forensic examiners since the security mechanisms are sometimes quite different.

item.title: ToDO
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bmJSWrVuIVQ https://mobile-security.gitbook.io/mobile-security-testing-guide/ios-testing-guide/0x06h-testing-platform-interaction cycript #todo*: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/network/implementing_netcat_with_network_framework

item.title: iOS Keychain
item.description: In this article I’m trying to study how keychain works. Metaphor There once lived a monkey 🐒 George.

item.title: Windows Forensics Plan
item.description: Plan for Windows Forensics Done (Udemy watched, tools installed and used, conclusions made) is bolded:
